title: Redesign dashboard around daily TUI ledger
description: Replace the production dashboard with a dense TUI daily usage ledger, retire mock routes, add focused daily-session backend support, and validate the redesigned dashboard.
close_reason: Implemented the production TUI dashboard redesign with date-descending daily ledger, selected-day sessions API, validation, and turn documentation.

title: reconcile dependent dirtydash prs
description: PR #3 was built on top of the current fast-mode chart/pricing branch. Merge or rebase the dependent PRs in the right order, resolve conflicts, validate, and push the final state.
close_reason: Merged PR #3 and PR #4 into the fast-mode chart branch, resolved dashboard and Beads conflicts, and preserved all PR changes.

title: Clarify fast-mode accounting for Codex usage
description: Dirtydash currently presents cached-input pricing as fast usage, which can understate or misrepresent Codex subscription drain when users run xhigh sessions mostly in fast mode. Investigate importer and UI labeling so fast-mode/token-limit accounting is distinguished from cache-read cost estimates.
close_reason: Added dashboard copy that separates Codex fast-mode subscription drain from cache-read/API-style cost estimates.

title: Resolve merge conflicts on PR #2
description: PR #2 is dirty against main. Resolve the merge conflicts on lavender/accounting-diff-investigation, validate the repository, document the turn if files change, and push the branch so the PR can merge cleanly.
close_reason: Resolved PR #2 merge conflicts by merging origin/main into lavender/accounting-diff-investigation, preserving both importer fixes in importers.rs, keeping Beads records from both sides, and validating with cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p dirtydash, and dashboard build.

title: Fix Codex token_count accounting
description: DirtyDash undercounts Codex costs because Codex token_count rows expose per-request usage in payload.info.last_token_usage, while the importer recursively extracts that value and then treats it as cumulative. Update the parser to explicitly read last_token_usage as the event increment, use total_token_usage only as a fallback/delta source, and add regression coverage against current Codex JSONL shape.
notes: Validation detail: temp import into /tmp/dirtydash-accounting-fixed.sqlite3 produced Codex 30d base-rate estimate $971.05 and all-time Codex base-rate estimate $1314.55 after the parser and cached-input split fix.
close_reason: Fixed Codex token_count accounting by reading payload.info.last_token_usage as the event increment, splitting cached input from non-cached input for Codex rows, and adding regression coverage. Validation: cargo test -p dirtydash; temp import produced Codex 30d base-rate estimate .05.

title: Price imported Codex model aliases correctly
description: DirtyDash is undercounting estimated spend because imported Codex model identifiers can land in zero-dollar/unpriced model rows even when they correspond to priced OpenAI models. Add model alias handling and validation so aggregated spend reflects those token rows.
close_reason: Fixed imported Codex/pi-agent spend undercounting by parsing nested usage, using source-reported costs, aliasing Codex/OpenAI pricing identifiers, updating stale imported rows on reimport, adding regressions, and updating the dashboard to surface unpriced tokens.

title: Scaffold dirtydash V1 local-first foundation
description: Build the initial V1 foundation from the approved plan: Rust CLI, SQLite storage, local importers, pricing overrides, embedded dashboard, validation tests, and turn documentation.
close_reason: Implemented the V1 local-first foundation: Rust CLI/workspace, SQLite storage, four local importers, pricing snapshot/overrides, SSH remote configuration with read-only discovery, embedded React/Vite dashboard, tests, validation, and turn documentation. Follow-up remote import and fixture hardening are tracked separately.

title: Bump version to v0.1.1
description: Update project package metadata to release version v0.1.1, validate the repository still builds, and document the change.
close_reason: Version metadata bumped to v0.1.1 and validated with cargo test plus dashboard build.

title: Fix dashboard graph hover and time direction
description: The dashboard graph should show mouseover details only while hovering, and the current day should sit on the right so time reads left to right.
close_reason: Fixed chart tooltip hover behavior, chronological chart order, y-axis labels, and selected-day sessions spacing.

title: create tui-inspired mockup routes
description: Add four developer-focused mockup routes for the current app that explore an enhanced TUI-leaning web UX without card-heavy dashboard conventions.
close_reason: Added four TUI-inspired mockup routes with hotkeys, responsive panes, validation, and turn documentation.

title: Make deploy script interactive
description: Update the dirtydash deploy helper to prompt for local versus remote deployment when no mode flag is supplied, and update docs/turn record accordingly.
close_reason: Deploy script now prompts for local versus remote mode, keeps explicit mode flags, and docs are updated.

title: Add dirtydash deployment script and docs
description: Add a documented script for deploying dirtydash updates either from a local machine over SSH or directly on the server. Include usage docs and validation notes.
close_reason: Added deploy script, deployment docs, validation, and turn record.

title: restore fast mode usage in dashboard graphs
description: Fast/priority mode usage is no longer visible after recent dashboard changes. Integrate fast mode usage amounts into the existing model, token usage, pricing, and related charts with a clear yellow encoding.
close_reason: restored fast/priority usage repair and chart highlighting

title: Mirror CodexBar fork baseline handling for Codex token rows
description: Dirtydash now detects archived Codex sessions and aligns priority pricing, but CodexBar also handles forked sessions, parent-session inherited totals, and divergent cumulative token_count rows. Add fork-aware Codex delta parsing so remaining Dirtydash/CodexBar reconciliation deltas can be attributed to date windows or cache freshness instead of parser semantics.

title: show gpt-5.5 fast model totals separately
description: The dashboard model totals are not showing gpt-5.5-fast even though recent gpt-5.5 usage was in fast mode. Investigate model normalization and pricing so fast-mode usage is preserved and displayed correctly.
close_reason: Codex low-effort GPT 5.5 and GPT 5.4 usage now imports as fast model slugs, with regression coverage and local re-import validation.

title: Consolidate model slugs and fast mode usage
description: Normalize provider/model aliases so different harnesses roll up to canonical model rows while keeping fast-mode slugs as distinct model rows. Align bundled pricing semantics with Cursor's models and pricing table where practical, especially treating cache write as part of input/cache-write pricing rather than an extra GPT-only write surcharge.
close_reason: Implemented model-only display labels, kept fast-mode slugs separate, refreshed Cursor-aligned bundled pricing, and added regression coverage.

title: Clarify cache metrics when writes are not reported
description: The cache dashboard currently labels cached-input share as a cache hit ratio and shows cache writes even when local session logs do not expose write lifecycle data. Update the UI copy so users understand Dirtydash reports observed cache reads and only shows write tokens when a source actually reports them.
close_reason: Clarified cache read share semantics in the dashboard and API, documented cache-write limitations, and validated build/test/browser smoke checks.

title: Update README roadmap checklist
description: Revise README so the project name remains lowercase dirtydash, move the project roadmap up to the current-state area, combine overlapping current-state and roadmap content where it reads better, and present the roadmap as a checklist.
close_reason: Updated README to keep dirtydash lowercase, merged Current State with the project roadmap near the top, converted roadmap milestones into a checklist, and removed the duplicate lower roadmap section.

title: Update README project roadmap
description: Replace the README roadmap with the provided full roadmap, customized to match the repository's current V1 foundation and terminology.
close_reason: Updated README with the supplied Project Roadmap, customized V1 to match the current repository state, and folded current limitations into roadmap status.

title: Document dirtydash README
description: Create a README that explains the dirtydash project, its current state, and roadmap in terms that match the existing V1 shape.
close_reason: Created a root README documenting dirtydash, the current V1 foundation, usage commands, limitations, and the forward roadmap.

title: Model Codex Fast and long-context pricing
description:
DirtyDash now parses Codex token_count rows correctly for base-rate accounting, but final totals still diverge from CodexBar/TokScale: current base-rate import was ~$971.05 (last 30d) and ~$1,314.55 (all-time) in a clean reimport, while CodexBar still reports ~$1,241 last-30d and TokScale reports ~$1.6k all-time.

Add support for Codex service-tier and context-window pricing so the remaining gap is explainable and matchable.

What to inspect:
- Source JSONL fields that indicate tiering, e.g. model naming suffixes/prefixes, metadata labels, or dedicated fields that map to "fast" and "priority" processing.
- Long-context markers (input/output token bands, model family, or row-level flags) that should switch to elevated per-token rates.
- How CodexBar decides whether to apply surcharge tiers for each event (per-session vs per-request, and whether cached tokens participate).
- Whether surcharge/long-context pricing is based on model alias, window crossing thresholds, or a fixed uplift for certain rows.
- Whether any post-processing in CodexBar is applied per model family after import.

Required implementation work:
1) Add new pricing metadata keys for Codex fast/priority and long-context rates.
2) Update importer to persist codex tier/context attributes on parsed rows so cost math can be applied deterministically.
3) Extend pricing resolver to apply tiered rates and add regression tests for each branch (fast, priority, long-context, and cached-token behavior).
4) Add reporting that surfaces base-rate vs fast/priority/long-context components separately in aggregate views and per-session breakdowns.
5) Reconcile by running a temp import and document: imported-cost, CodexBar last-30d, all-time baseline, and a delta explanation table.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: dirtydash can compute Codex costs with three clearly attributable buckets: base-rate, fast/priority surcharge, and long-context surcharge. Implement deterministic mapping from payload/model hints to service tier and threshold. Add tests for fast vs non-fast, priority, long-context and cached tokens so deltas close to CodexBar/TokScale are explainable. Update docs/labels so users can inspect which rows used each rate.
close_reason: implemented codexbar-style codex pricing modes, local logs_2 priority evidence, priority aggregate splits, shared fast usage bars, pricing-page usage bars, tests, visual checks, and turn documentation. broader codexbar 30-day reconciliation remains tracked by dirtydash-q9g.

title: Reconcile DirtyDash estimated spend with CodexBar 30-day total
description: DirtyDash now prices previously zero-dollar Codex/pi-agent rows by importing nested usage and reported costs, but a clean temp import totals about $456.81 while CodexBar reports about $1204 for the last 30 days. Investigate CodexBar's exact data source, date window, and pricing semantics so DirtyDash can explain or match the remaining gap.
acceptance_criteria: DirtyDash can show a reconciliation view or documented calculation that explains the difference from CodexBar's last-30-days total, including source coverage, date-window boundaries, and pricing assumptions.
close_reason: aligned Dirtydash Codex source coverage, priority pricing, and dashboard token summaries with CodexBar; filed dirtydash-ejj for remaining fork-baseline nuance

title: Harden importers with real-world source fixtures
description: Collect representative Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and pi-agent fixtures from real installations and tighten parsers around edge cases that are not covered by the synthetic V1 scaffold tests.

title: Import usage files from SSH remotes
description: Extend V1 remote sync beyond read-only file discovery so it can pull or stream known JSON/JSONL usage files over SSH/SFTP, import them into SQLite, and preserve remote machine provenance for each usage event.
